In terms of death, what was the number needed to treat for Nomort versus chemotherapy over 5 years?

questions.explanation: Anti-Jo1 is associated with anti-synthetase syndrome, including mechanics hands, inflammatory arthritis, ILD, and myositis. Anti-Scl 70 is usually associated with diffuse systemic sclerosis which is associated with ILD. Although myositis can occur, this is usually mild. Anti-MDA5 is associated with ILD (which can be rapidly progressive), and characteristic cutaneous changes, such as ulcerations; up to 50% of patients may not show signs of myositis. Anti-SRP is associated with a necrotising autoi
questions.stem: A 72 year old female ex-smoker presents with a 6 month history of progressive difficulty with ascending from bed and chairs, getting dressed and hanging out washing. There has been weight loss of 2kg and mild dyspnoea on exertion. A CT of the chest shows predominant emphysematous changes. Examination shows 3/5 proximal lower limb power and 4/5 proximal upper limb power. CK level is 5000, with AST 150 and ALT 50. CRP is 30 and ESR 65. MRI shows atrophy of vastus muscles with scattered increased muscle signal in both the vastus muscles. Which of the following antibodies is most likely to be associated with his disease?
Azathioprine is a type of thiopurine used in the management of inflammatory bowel disease. Which one of the following markers would indicate reduced thiopurine metabolism?
questions.explanation: Reduced thiopurine metabolism is primarily indicated by genetic markers for the enzymes involved in its breakdown, leading to characteristic changes in blood cell counts and metabolite levels. Reduced thiopurine metabolism, specifically due to low Thiopurine S-methyltransferase (TPMT) activity, is indicated by high 6-thioguanine nucleotide (6-TGN) levels and low or undetectable 6- methylmercaptopurine (6-MMP) levels. Pre-treatment genetic testing is commonly used to identify individuals at risk.

questions.stem: A couple would like advice on the risk of cystic fibrosis for their current pregnancy. The male partner's brother is affected with cystic fibrosis (CF) as indicated in the above pedigree. The carrier frequency for CF in the general population is about 1 in 25. What is the estimated risk of CF in the pregnancy?
questions.explanation: As CF is an autosomal recessive condition, the male partner (the father-to-be) has a 2/3 chance of being a carrier for CF. Both his parents are assumed to be carriers, and therefore when his parents had children, each pregnancy had a 1 in 4 chance of being affected, a 1 in 2 chance of being a carrier and a 1 in 4 chance of having two normal copies of the gene. However, as you already know the male partner is unaffected, the chance that he is a carrier is 2/3, as you only count the 3 remaining po

questions.stem:
Dual anti-platelet therapy is standard practice for acute coronary syndrome.
Select the best description of the properties of the P2Y12 inhibitors' onset of effect and reversibility.

options.text: Clopidogrel: 2 hours & irreversible; Ticagrelor: 30 minutes & reversible; Prasugrel: 30 minutes & irreversible
Clopidogrel: 4 hours & reversible; Ticagrelor: 4 hours & irreversible; Prasugrel: 4 hours & irreversible
Clopidogrel: 12 hours & irreversible; Ticagrelor: 30 minutes & irreversible; Prasugrel: 12 hours & irreversible
Clopidogrel: 12 hours & irreversible; Ticagrelor: 12 hours & reversible; Prasugrel: 30 minutes & reversible

questions.explanation: Evinacumab's mechanism of action is to inhibit angiopoietin-like protein 3 (ANGPTL3), which increases lipoprotein lipase activity, leading to increased clearance of triglyceride-rich lipoproteins and reduced LDL-C levels.

options.text: Uses RNA interference to silence the gene for proprotein convertase subtilisin/kexin type 9
Binds to and blocks the protein angiopoietin-like protein 3
The activated form inhibits ATP citrate lyase thereby blocking a key step in the cholesterol biosynthesis pathway

questions.stem:
For patients with familial hypercholesterolaemia, the initial treatment is a high-intensity statin. Second-line treatment can consist of ezetimibe and/or a PCSK9 inhibitor. For treatment-refractory homozygous familial hypercholesterolaemia evinacumab can be considered where clinical criteria are met.

What is true regarding evinacumab?

questions.explanation: VEXAS is a classic case - older man, myelodysplasia and relapsing polychondritis. UBA1 is the somatically mutated gene in VEXAS. Factor 5 Leiden is for DVTs, pyrin for FMF, and CFTR for CF.
questions.stem:
A 72 year old man has had myelodysplasia (refractory cytopenias) for 18 months. He has required no treatment. He now presents with a tender red left ear and a tender nasal bridge. You suspect a genetic disorder.

The most appropriate gene test to consider is:

options.text: Severe Chronic Rhinosinusitis with Nasal Polyps: IL4/13: Dupilumab

questions.stem: Match the correct condition: pathway/immunologic mediators: drug

A 25-year-old man wants to increase his muscle mass to improve his sport performance. He attempts to accomplish this by injections of a substance obtained from a friend. Over the next year he develops acne, gynaecomastia and increased fatigue. Laboratory studies show Hb 175 g/L (Reference Range 130-170), Hct 0.55L/L (Reference Range 0.36-0.55).

Which of the following complications is he at greatest risk for developing in the future?
questions.explanation: Several reports describe sudden death in young athletes who had no previously known heart disease but who were taking androgens; cardiac hypertrophy or myocarditis were found at autopsy. Anabolic steroids induce left ventricular hypertrophy. Among men who stop using androgens, sperm count usually returns to normal within four months.
Oral bioavailability = (AUC_oral/AUC_iv) x (Dose_iv/Dose_oral) = (150/100) x (20/50) = 1.5 x 0.4 = 0.6 = 60%
questions.stem: In a subject taking part in a bioavailability study, the area under the plasma concentration versus time curve (AUC) achieved after an intravenous dose of 20mg is 100mg/hr/L, compared with an AUC of 150 mg/hr/L after an oral dose of 50 mg. The oral bioavailability of the drug in this subject is:
A single base substitution has altered a triplet codon in the reading frame from CAG to UAG. Referring to the triplet codon table provided, this variant can be best described as:
questions.explanation: CAG codes for glutamine. UAG is a stop codon. A single base substitution changing an amino acid codon to a stop codon is a nonsense variant.

questions.stem:
Julie is a 19-year-old female who presents to the Emergency Department with a seizure. She is post-ictal and drowsy. Her friend gives collateral history that she has known epilepsy, is compliant with her medications, and has not had a seizure for many years. On further questioning the friend admits she gave Julie her oral contraceptive pills. You suspect a drug interaction.

What anti-epileptic medication do you suspect Julie was taking?
questions.explanation: Oestrogen-containing oral contraceptives can reduce lamotrigine levels by up to 50% through induction of UGT enzymes, potentially triggering breakthrough seizures.

questions.stem:
Choose the BEST initial treatment or management option to control the patient's symptoms in the following scenario:

A 34-year-old woman with poorly controlled generalised epilepsy on lamotrigine and topiramate presents with recurrent tonic-clonic seizures over the last 40 minutes. Each of the episodes lasts between 3 and 6 minutes. There is no return of consciousness in between episodes.
questions.explanation: This is status epilepticus (continuous seizure activity >5 min or recurrent seizures without recovery of consciousness). First-line treatment is a benzodiazepine. Clonazepam is the benzodiazepine option available.

options.text: No anti-epileptic therapy, monitor and initiate therapy if further seizure
No anti-epileptic therapy - this is a non-epileptiform disorder

questions.explanation: CDK4 and CDK6 control the progression of the cell cycle from G1 to S phase. Option A describes aromatase inhibitors. Option C describes PARP inhibitors. Option D describes monoclonal antibodies like trastuzumab.

options.text: Blocks the repair of single-strand DNA breaks
Binds to a transmembrane glycoprotein receptor

questions.stem: Cyclin-dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitors (CDK4/6 inhibitors) are commonly used in the management of hormone receptor-positive breast cancer. What is their mechanism of function?

questions.explanation: The common peroneal nerve innervates tibialis anterior, extensor digitorum longus, extensor hallucis longus and fibularis longus. Injury causes loss of dorsiflexion, eversion, hallux extension and toe extension, with preservation of inversion. Option A is consistent with sciatic nerve injury or L5 radiculopathy. Option C is S1 radiculopathy. Option D is consistent with superficial fibular (peroneal) nerve injury.

options.text: Loss of dorsiflexion, inversion, and toe extension
Loss of dorsiflexion, eversion, and toe extension
Loss of plantarflexion and ankle inversion
Loss of eversion only with preserved dorsiflexion

questions.stem:
A 39-year-old man presents to the emergency department with a 1 week history of difficulty walking. This was preceded by 1-2 months of malaise, fatigue, arthralgias and occasional fevers. He is otherwise healthy and does not take any regular medications. Examination shows asymmetrical lower limb weakness, worse on the right. Nerve conduction studies confirm an axonal pattern of loss in the common peroneal nerve.

Which of the following examination findings would be consistent with his nerve conduction study results?
Which of the following findings is most likely to be found at autopsy in frontotemporal degeneration-ALS?
questions.explanation: TDP-43 protein is abnormally phosphorylated and ubiquitinated neuronal cytoplasmic inclusions seen in FTD-ALS. Aβ42 peptide is in neuritic plaques in Alzheimer disease. Argyrophilic-positive inclusions are tau inclusions in amnestic MCI and behavioral variant FTD. Lewy bodies are alpha-synuclein inclusions in Lewy body dementia.

Loading dose = Vd x target concentration = 0.8 L/kg x 50 kg x 25 mg/L = 1000 mg. Infusion rate = Clearance x target concentration = 30 ml/min x 25 mg/L = 0.03 L/min x 25 mg/L = 0.75 mg/min = 45 mg/hr. Using 40 mg/hr achieves steady state between 20-25 mg/L.
questions.stem:
A 50 kg patient in ICU is critically ill and you wish to rapidly achieve a steady state concentration of vancomycin 20-25 mg/L with a bolus and then commence a continuous infusion to maintain this concentration. The Vd of vancomycin is 0.8 L/kg and the clearance of vancomycin for this patient is 30 ml/min.

What bolus dose and continuous infusion rate is required?

questions.explanation: CUG codes for Leucine and UUG also codes for Leucine - therefore this is a silent (synonymous) variant where the amino acid is unchanged despite the nucleotide substitution.
questions.stem: A single base substitution has altered a triplet codon in the reading frame from CUG to UUG. Referring to the triplet codon table provided, this variant can be best described as:

questions.explanation: Dupilumab targets IL-4/13 and is used for Severe Asthma and Chronic Rhinosinusitis with Nasal Polyps. Upadacitinib is a JAK inhibitor (not IL6). Tocilizumab is an IL-6 receptor inhibitor. Omalizumab targets IgE, not IL4/13.

options.text: Severe Asthma and Chronic Rhinosinusitis with Nasal Polyps: IL4/13: Dupilumab

questions.explanation: E. faecium is naturally (intrinsically) resistant to cephalosporins. This trait is encoded by chromosomal determinants in the core genome of these organisms.
questions.stem: Which one of these bacteria would NOT respond to cephalosporins?

A 65-year-old lifelong smoker is referred to lung nodule clinic for assessment. He reports a six-month history of 6kg of unintentional weight loss with occasional small volume haemoptysis, fatigue and intermittent night sweats. An outpatient CT chest demonstrates mild centrilobular emphysema with a 20mm spiculated solid lung nodule in the anterior segment of the right upper lobe. His ECOG status is 1 with an exercise tolerance of 500m.

Which is correct with regards to the diagnosis of this lesion?
questions.explanation: The VERITAS study (NEJM May 2025) demonstrated navigational bronchoscopy biopsy resulted in accurate diagnosis in 79% vs 73.6% for transthoracic needle biopsy (p=0.003 for non-inferiority, p=0.17 for superiority). Pneumothorax occurred in 3.3% vs 28.3% respectively (p<0.001).

options.text: A diagnostic procedure involving navigational bronchoscopy to biopsy this lesion is more likely to lead to a pneumothorax than a transthoracic needle biopsy
A Prostate Specific Membrane Antigen Positron Emission Tomography scan would be the next investigation
The diagnostic accuracy of a procedure involving navigational bronchoscopy to biopsy this lesion is non-inferior to transthoracic needle biopsy
The diagnostic accuracy of a procedure involving navigational bronchoscopy to biopsy this lesion is superior to transthoracic needle biopsy

questions.stem:
For each of the following presentations, select the most likely diagnosis:

A 24-year-old male schoolteacher presents to his GP with a 1-week history of coryzal symptoms. He reports a 5-day history of dark coloured urine not associated with pain. On examination, there is minor bilateral pitting pedal oedema. Urinalysis showed 1+ protein and 3+ blood. Blood tests demonstrate serum creatinine 80 umol/L (RR <110) with eGFR>90mL/min.
questions.explanation: IgA nephropathy is the most common cause of glomerulonephritis. A classical presentation involves synpharyngitic haematuria (haematuria occurring concurrently with upper respiratory tract infection).
A is the left main stem. C is the left circumflex coronary artery. D is the right coronary artery.
questions.stem: Which of the following letters represents the left anterior descending coronary artery?
Which of the following conditions predisposes to the highest risk of colorectal cancer?
questions.explanation: Risk of CRC in patients with UC and PSC is high and can occur early. Annual surveillance colonoscopies are recommended at time of PSC diagnosis. Increased inflammation also increases cancer risk.

options.text: Crohn's disease only affecting the terminal ileum
Ulcerative colitis with primary sclerosing cholangitis
Ulcerative colitis with pancolitis which is in endoscopic and histological remission

questions.explanation: X-linked SCID due to common-gamma chain deficiency results in T-B+NK- immunophenotype. The common-gamma chain is a component of IL-2, IL-4, IL-7, IL-9, IL-15 and IL-21 receptors. IL-7 is essential for T cell development, IL-15 for NK cell development.
questions.stem: X-linked common-gamma chain deficiency results in which of the following immunophenotypes?
An 82 year old woman living independently in the community has a recent fall with no significant injuries. Which of the following interventions is most effective at reducing her risk of future falls?
questions.explanation: Exercise programs, especially those including balance and strength training, are one of the most effective interventions for reducing falls risk in community-dwelling elderly.

Stage 3 pressure injuries involve full thickness skin loss with adipose tissue visible at the base. Stage 1 is non-blanchable erythema of intact skin. Stage 2 is partial thickness loss with exposed dermis. Stage 4 involves full thickness loss with exposed bone, tendon or muscle.
questions.stem:
William, a 78 year old man in the hospital, is found to have a pressure injury. On examination, there appears to be full thickness skin loss with adipose tissue visible at the base of the wound.

What stage of pressure injury is present?

questions.explanation: Hyperplastic polyps have a low risk of malignant transformation and do not require follow up if <10mm. High risk polyps include adenomas larger than 10mm, adenomas with tubulovillous histology, or adenomas with high grade dysplasia. Serrated high risk polyps include sessile serrated >10mm, sessile serrated with dysplasia or traditional serrated adenoma.

options.text: Tubular adenoma with low grade dysplasia
Tubulovillous adenoma with low grade dysplasia

questions.stem: Which polyp carries the highest likelihood of malignancy?
